senses.definition: A family of small beetles that bore into the bark of trees, often causing damage to forests and fruits.
examples: Scolytidae are commonly known as bark beetles.
Forests suffered heavy damage from scolytidae infestations.

senses.definition: Plural form referring to members of the Scolytidae family, i.e., bark beetles.
examples: The scolytids attacked stressed pines.
Researchers study scolytids to protect forests.

senses.definition: Relating to or resembling bark beetles (Scolytidae) or their characteristics.
examples: Scolytoid insects share features with bark beetles.
The scolytoid family includes several wood-boring species.

senses.definition: Plural form referring to insects in the sclotoid grouping; bark beetles or closely related wood-boring beetles.
examples: Scolytoids are a diverse group of wood-boring beetles.
Several scoltoyoid species cause tree mortality.

senses.definition: A genus within the bark beetle family Scolytidae, containing many wood-boring species.
examples: Scolytus is a genus that includes important bark beetles.
Researchers studied Scolytus specimens from infected trees.

senses.definition: A genus of fast-moving marine fish including many mackerel species.
examples: Scomber is known for its streamlined body and speed.
Many sa people fish for scomber in coastal waters.

senses.definition: Resembling or related to scombers (mackerel-like in shape or habit).
examples: The scomberoid fish displayed a swift, torpedo-like form.
Researchers classify the creature as scomberoid due to its features.

senses.definition: A family name formerly used for a group of scombroid fishes; the taxonomic classification has changed over time.
examples: Scombresocidae was once used for related scombroid fishes.
Taxonomy has reclassified many scombroid groups since then.

senses.definition: An extinct genus of prehistoric scombroid fishes, known from fossil records.
examples: Scombresox fossils provide clues about ancient marine life.
Paleontologists study scombresox to understand early scombroids.

senses.definition: A fish belonging to the Scombridae family, such as mackerel or tuna.
examples: Scombrids are prized for their lean flesh.
Tuna is a well-known scombrid.

senses.definition: A family of fishes including most mackerels, tunas, and related species.
examples: Scombridae includes tuna and mackerel.
Fishermen target scombridae for market value.

senses.definition: Plural for fishes in the Scombridae family, such as mackerel and tuna.
examples: Scombrids are fast-swimming predators.
Catches of scombrids are common in the season.

senses.definition: Resembling or having the form of scombrids (mackerel-like in shape).
examples: The scombriform body is streamlined for fast swimming.
Scombriform silhouettes are common in many tuna species.

senses.definition: An order or grouping including many scombrid-like fishes; often used to refer to taxonomic groups related to mackerels and tunas.
examples: Scombriformes include several fast-swimming species.
Classification within scombriformes has changed over time.

senses.definition: A toxin produced by certain scombrid fishes, particularly in some spoiled fish products; also refers to a type of toxin related to histamine formation in scombroid fish poisoning.
examples: Scombrine compounds can contribute to food safety concerns.
Scombroid poisoning occurs when scombrine-containing fish are consumed.

senses.definition: A type of food poisoning caused by histamine produced in spoiled fish of the scombriform group; also refers to the fish producing this toxin and to the condition itself.
examples: Scombroid poisoning is also called histamine fish poisoning.
Improper storage of reef fish can lead to scombroid illness.

senses.definition: A suborder or superfamily grouping that includes many scombroid fish-related species; used in taxonomic contexts.
examples: Scombroidea contains several families of fast-swimming fish.
Taxonomists debate the boundaries of Scombroidea.

senses.definition: Relating to Scombroidea or to scombroid fishes; resembling members of the Scombridae family.
examples: A scombroidean fish features a streamlined body.
Researchers study scombroidean traits in pelagic species.

senses.definition: Fishes belonging to the Scombroidea group; commonly used to refer to scombrids and related species.
examples: Scombroids include many tuna and mackerel species.
Markets sell various scombroids around the coast.

senses.definition: A term historically used for certain scombroid fishes; sometimes used as an old common name for some tuna-like species.
examples: Scombrone appears in older nautical references.
Common names like scombrone vary by region.

senses.definition: To catch or harvest scombrids (mackerel-like fishes) for food or commerce.
examples: Fishermen scomfish the coastal waters at dawn.
Markets rely on communities that scomfish the sea.

senses.definition: Past tense of scomfish; caught scombrid fish.
examples: They scomfished the morning catch near the harbor.
The crew scomfished several tuna yesterday.

senses.definition: Present tense or base form meaning to catch scombrid fish; also used as plural noun for scombridae fishes in some dialects.
examples: They scomfishes along the coast every weekend.
Scomfishes are sold fresh in the market.

senses.definition: The activity or practice of catching scombrids; fishing for tuna/mackerel-like fishes.
examples: Scomfishing is a major industry in coastal regions.
The season for scomfishing runs from spring to autumn.

senses.definition: A traditional confection or candy, sometimes named from historical syrups; also a pellet-like candy in some contexts.
examples: The shop offered an old-fashioned scomfit.
Scomfit recipes vary by region.
